stack_note: Vanilla canvas: tap-to-drop destruction sim over a realistic photo cityscape with a procedural top-down blast/crater/mushroom system; no physics engine, so Phaser adds nothing. Next ARCADE/physics standalone should go Phaser to rebalance the A/B portfolio.

idea.title: Megaton
idea.hook: Drop ONE nuke on an isometric block city (City Smash style); watch the blast wave blow the block-buildings apart into flying chunks, scorch the ground, and raise a mushroom cloud; earn from the destruction and upgrade power until a single nuke levels the whole city, which then rebuilds bigger.
idea.prototype: Nuclear destruction sandbox over an ISOMETRIC BLOCK CITY (separate block-buildings on a street grid, varied heights, lit windows; pure procedural iso - reference: City Smash / Total City Smash / Teardown voxel destruction). Boot straight in with a pulsing 'TAP THE CITY TO DROP THE NUKE' crosshair, a faint blast-radius ellipse showing the current destruction SIZE, and a bottom bar [AIR BURST | UNDERGROUND | Upgrade Power]. ONE nuke per drop: pick delivery (AIR = wide blast; UNDERGROUND = penetrator, smaller radius, +25% money), tap the city to drop there. The warhead falls (or burrows), detonates: white flash, an expanding blast WAVE that ripples out across the iso grid and makes each building it reaches EXPLODE - the building bursts into flying chunk-blocks (capped/pooled physics: tumble out + arc down) and collapses to rubble, leaving a scorched crater on the ground + ember ring; a mushroom cloud rises, screen shake, deep boom. Money = buildings destroyed (by height/type x tier); a DESTROYED% bar fills; at 94% the city is LEVELED -> 'CITY LEVELED +$payout' -> Rebuild & Nuke restores a BIGGER city (Town -> City -> Metropolis -> Megacity -> ... bigger iso grid each tier). Spend $ to Upgrade Power -> bigger blast radius (the preview ellipse visibly grows); ~power 9 one-nukes a whole city (the milestone Tim asked for). Endless idle, no fail, persistent save.
idea.mutation_of: City-destruction sandbox (City Smash / Total City Smash: Nuclear War, Teardown-style voxel destruction) compressed into a tap-to-drop iso block city with an idle power-upgrade loop and an air-vs-underground delivery choice.
idea.why_novel: No destruction sandbox in the catalog. The isometric block-building city that physically explodes into flying chunks under a visible blast radius + a power-upgrade-to-one-nuke loop is distinct from our merge/idle/clicker games.
idea.non_timer_pressure: no timer. Drivers: the per-city WIPE goal (destroy 90%, visible DESTROYED% bar) + aim (small early blasts reward hitting undestroyed area) + delivery choice (wide AIR coverage vs concentrated UNDERGROUND +money) + the power-upgrade economy (rising costs gate how fast your blast grows toward one-nuke).
idea.lose_condition: none - endless incremental sim (idle class, like 167_cedar_hollow); structure = per-city wipe goal + power economy; gs never enters GAMEOVER

sources.title: Nuclear war prompt -> destruction sim (refined)
sources.snippet: From Tim's nuclear-war prompt. After a busy turn-based duel and a first destruction-clicker were rejected ('animation and city are very bad'), Tim specified: 'realistic city, player has only one nuke, he can choose to put it underground or throw from the air. the nuke destroy, after each drop he gets some money to increase the power of nuke until able to 1 nuke whole city.' Built to that spec with an AI-generated photoreal cityscape + top-down nuke destruction.

synthesizer_brief: Vanilla-JS canvas destruction sim over a photoreal aerial cityscape image (AI-gen, procedural fallback), mobile-portrait-first. One nuke per drop with an AIR/UNDERGROUND delivery toggle; tap the city to drop; top-down shockwave + scorch crater (chars the photo to rubble) + rising mushroom cloud + flash/shake/boom. Destruction grid -> money + DESTROYED%; 90% = CITY DESTROYED payout -> bigger next city (art cycles). Bottom bar: AIR | UNDERGROUND | Upgrade Power. Power grows the blast until one nuke wipes a whole city. Endless idle, persistent save, EN+RU, gf-lib juice/audio.
